summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of SafeNet MVP. Backend: 28/29 pytest assertions passed (single failure is a test race condition in parallel workers between quiz CRUD and admin_stats). Frontend: all critical user flows verified end-to-end — Home, Scam Types (11), Safety Tips (10), Quiz, Report, Contact, Blog (5), Search, Theme toggle, AI Chat streaming, AI Detect verdict, Register + auto-login, Login as admin -> /admin redirect, non-admin blocked from /admin, Admin dashboard (Overview stats + all 6 tabs with real data), Report status change, Users list. All AI calls hit real Gemini API and returned successfully in <10s during tests.

minor.issue: TestAdminAuth::test_admin_stats occasionally fails when TestAdminCRUD::test_quiz_crud is executing in a parallel worker (quiz question count is 16 during the race window). Not a backend bug — a test isolation concern only. Consider running admin_stats test in a fixture that snapshots counts or serializing writes.

issues: Two harmless console warnings from Radix DialogContent about missing aria-describedby (accessibility hint — not a functional bug).

AuthContext calls /api/auth/me on every fresh visit which produces a 401 in the console when the user is logged-out. This is expected behavior but noisy; could be suppressed by using a lightweight response validator or 204 for unauth.

action_items: OPTIONAL: eliminate the flaky test race by running admin_stats before CRUD tests (or add pytest-order marker). Backend itself is fully functional.
OPTIONAL: quiet the /auth/me 401 console noise by only calling /me if a session cookie exists, or by treating 401 as normal state without console.error (axios can pass validateStatus).
OPTIONAL: add aria-describedby (or a DialogDescription) to all Radix Dialogs to remove the Radix a11y warning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py cookie samesite=none + secure=True is correct for cross-site preview; but consider adding a /api/auth/refresh endpoint that uses the refresh_token — currently a refresh token is set but never consumed anywhere.
AI /api/ai/chat writes only the assistant message to DB after streaming completes, so if the client disconnects the assistant reply is lost — acceptable, but worth documenting.
server.py:264 — CHAT ignores role check; anyone (unauthenticated) can spam expensive Gemini requests. Consider adding a soft rate-limit or requiring auth for /ai/chat and /ai/detect.
server.py:418 update_report_status accepts raw dict `body: dict` — safer to declare a Pydantic model with a Literal type for status.

Frontend Register.js does NOT explicitly refetch /auth/me after setUser; works because register endpoint returns the user object, but if the returned shape ever changes it will silently break. Consider always doing api.get('/auth/me') after auth actions.
No file-size limit / MIME validation on Report screenshot upload beyond the 3MB backend cap; base64 payloads swell fast — the frontend already limits to 2MB client-side which is good.
seed_data.py is not shown but conditionally seeded — ensure blog_posts and scam_types slugs are unique (currently no unique index).
test_credentials: admin@safenet.com / SafeNet@Admin2026 (verified working). Created test users via register: pwuser2_hmcszk@safenet.com and others (all with password User@1234).
seed_data_creation: During testing, several TEST_ prefixed reports/contacts were POSTed as functional tests. Test users pwuser2_*@safenet.com left in DB for future runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The pytest race condition on test_admin_stats can be avoided by running pytest with `-p no:xdist` or `--dist=no`, or by adding pytest-ordering to force test_admin_stats to run before test_quiz_crud. Backend AI endpoints occasionally return 502 if Gemini upstream is overloaded (test_detect_scam has pytest.skip for that). All frontend testid patterns verified: report-row-{id}, message-row-{id}, user-row-{email}, stat-card-{key}, scam-add-button, admin-tab-{overview|scams|quiz|reports|messages|users}, chat-input, chat-send-button, chat-assistant-message, detect-message-input, detect-analyze-button, detect-verdict, detect-risk-score, quiz-option-N, quiz-explanation, quiz-next-button, register-confirm-input (NOT register-confirm-password-input).
rca of the issue: N/A — no P0/P1 bugs found. All features listed in the spec are working correctly.
